summary: Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to clear a Russian-held landing, offering surrender before striking when no response came. On March 14, 2026, Ukrainian kamikaze drones hit the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai, triggering a major fire. The SIGNUM battalion destroyed a Russian tank camouflaged with netting using an FPV strike. Russian forces countered Ukrainian drones with the "Yolka" anti-drone system, targeting fixed-wing "Hornet" UAVs. Ukrainian FPV tactics included blinding targets with smoke, using AI-powered HX-2 drones, and training AI models with frontline data. Russian infantry faced novel threats, including being charged by FPV drones and ambushed by coordinated strikes.
